title: tetragastris mucronata
intro: Tetragastris mucronata, also known as the Bitterwood or White Bitterwood, is a tropical evergreen tree native to the Caribbean and Central America, belonging to the family Clusiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Tetragastris mucronata include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it accessible for herbal remedies, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
